texto: I want to believe him. I know stuff happens to people and they can't always be who they were or who they think they're supposed to be. But knowing that doesn't mean I'm okay with it. It's more like what Mr. Krueger says about black holes: We can't wish them away, so we'd better learn as much as we can about where they are and how they work so we don't get sucked in.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a R. Dean Johnson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I want to believe him. I know stuff happens to people and they can't always be who they were or w…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, coping, depression pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e depression.

frase_original: I want to believe him. I know stuff happens to people and they can't always be who they were or who they think they're supposed to be. But knowing that doesn't mean I'm okay with it. It's more like what Mr. Krueger says about black holes: We can't wish them away, so we'd better learn as much as we can about where they are and how they work so we don't get sucked in.
